memories.content:
## MajorBBS Dual-Write Architecture — DAT Primary + SQLite Mirror

User proposed and agreed to a dual-write architecture for MajorBBS: keep native Btrieve DAT files as the authoritative primary storage, while maintaining SQLite as a read-only reporting copy. This approach:

- Preserves DAT files as the canonical data source (Btrieve-native compatibility)
- Enables SQLite for reporting/analytics without risking data integrity
- Allows users to query SQLite while MajorBBS operates on DAT files
- Provides a safer alternative to full real-time dual-write complexity

Implementation path: Build a DLL that handles both DAT operations (via wbtrv32 emulation) and maintains SQLite as a synchronized read-only mirror. This was the final decision after exploring reverse-conversion tools.

## MajorBBS Tooling Decision — Option A + Bidirectional DLL

User approved "Option A" approach for MajorBBS data migration tooling and confirmed requirement to build a drop-in DLL reverser. The solution will:

1. Support forward migration (DAT → SQLite) for reporting
2. Support reverse migration (SQLite → DAT) for rollback scenarios
3. Be packaged as a drop-in DLL that users can test and revert easily
4. Allow users to switch back to native Btrieve operation without data loss

This dual-mode capability addresses user concerns about migration safety and provides operational flexibility for MajorBBS deployments.
